response_constraints: Resolve the initial description once, then reuse the returned stable ID for both later updates.
Do not search again or switch entities after stage 1.
Treat the correction date as superseding the first requested date.
Never update either distractor record and never send a notification.
Report only facts established by tool results.
If multiple exact matches appear, ask the user to choose by ID before issuing an update.

stages.purpose: Resolve the user’s description to one stable ID before any mutation.
Reuse the ID established in stage 1 for the first requested date change.
Apply the correction to the same entity without searching again or touching distractors.

follow_up_turns.content: Please reschedule that same application to 2026-11-24. Do not touch the similarly named record and do not notify anyone.
Correction: I gave you the wrong date. Set that same application to 2026-11-26 instead; this replaces 2026-11-24. Reuse the same ID, do not search again, and do not notify anyone.

prompt: Find the application named “Street-vendor permit renewal” at Aurora and report its stable ID and current recorded date. This first turn is read-only; do not update it or notify anyone yet. If more than one exact name-and-location match appears, ask me to choose by ID before any later update.
